授業方法: This course includes face-to-face core lessons, as well as Start-up Week and Flex Hours on-demand lessons. Each core content lesson will be based on an adapted or originial academic English reading (about 8 - 12 pages). Lessons include a lecture with PowerPoint slides, student note-taking, and in-class lecture tasks. These are 3-4 short tasks which involve students thinking, speaking, and writing about lecture content in English. Task sheets will be given at the start of class, completed during class, and submitted at the end. These count towards students' performance grades.In addition to content lessons, three lessons will be used for Unit Tests. All readings will be distributed on LUNA. Students must access and use these electronic materials by themselves.

第1回:
"[Startup Week] Online class (on-demand format) / 《90 min》 Course guidance
See the course LUNA website for the link and materials.
第15回: [Classes and Examinations Period] Week 1 (Face-to-face class) / 《90 min》
トピック.授業計画1: Most weekly sessions will follow a similar pattern. Students will carefully read an academic English text and/or watch a media source on the topic of that week, then attend the in-person lecture, where they will take notes and complete short discussion and writing tasks. Three sessions are designated to complete the three Unit Tests.
トピック.授業外学習2:
Student preparation outside of class meetings includes:
(1) Complete required readings before lectures.
(2) Prepare for Unit Tests.

教科書/Required texts0: All reading materials will be provided by the instructor on LUNA. Students can expect approximately 8 - 12 pages of adapted and original English readings per week.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
Optional references:
Galloway, N. & Rose, H. (2015). Introducing Global Englishes. London: Routledge.
Jenkins, J. (2015). Global Englishes: A Resource Book for Students, 3rd Edition. London: Routledge.
Crystal, D. (2003). English as a Global Language, 2nd Edition. New York: Cambridge University Press.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: 各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
